advantages: Surfaces untested code paths you'd otherwise miss.
Branch coverage exposes unrun `else` / `except` paths that line coverage hides.
Diff coverage focuses attention on new/changed code instead of legacy gaps.
Trends over time alert you to regressions (refactor that orphaned tests).
CI gates create a baseline floor below which the codebase can't drift.
disadvantages: Coverage doesn't measure assert quality — high coverage can hide weak tests.
Coverage gates can be gamed (weak asserts to pass the gate).
100% targets discourage skipping trivial code that's not worth testing.
Adds CI time (especially with branch coverage on large codebases).
Generated / vendored code distorts the metric unless explicitly excluded.
